title: Number of Enclaves (Land Trapped by Sea)
description:
You are given a binary matrix where 1 represents land and 0 water. A land cell can walk to another by 4-direction moves. A land cell is an enclave if it cannot walk off the boundary of the grid. Return the count of land cells that are enclaves.

Constraints:
- 1 ≤ m, n ≤ 500
explanation:
Intuition
Flood-fill from boundary land to mark all land cells that can escape. The answer is total land minus escaped land.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Mark all boundary land with BFS/DFS
2. Main Algorithm: Spread to connected land marking as visited
3. Return Result: Count remaining unvisited land cells

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(mn)
- Space Complexity: O(mn)

Key Insights
- Reverse thinking: remove escaped land first
- Mutating grid to 0 is convenient

Common Pitfalls
- Be careful to only spread through land
- Do not count boundary-connected land

testCases.explanation: All land touches boundary via path
When num_enclaves(grid=[[0]]), the expected result is 0

hints: Start BFS from boundary land cells
